name: Interview / Dialog
description: Two-person dialog format. Two real people (or two AI-avatars) talk to each other on camera — expert+interviewer, two-friends-discussing, split-screen-reaction, or podcast-style sit-down with b-roll cutaways. 30-90s, 9:16. Reads less like one-person-yelling-at-the-camera and more like overhearing a real conversation, which is why retention beats single-speaker monologue. Distinct from `podcast-clip` (which cuts FROM a real long-form podcast) — this is for GENERATING a synthetic interview-style video from a written script with two distinct AI voices.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — TWO consistent character keyframes (one per speaker), reused as ref-image across every wan-25 turn so face/lighting/clothing stay stable across cuts
stackSummary.i2v: alibaba/wan-25 via OpenRouter — one call per dialog turn, audio-synced talking head; kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro for occasional b-roll cutaway (chart, product, screenshot) when the topic gets visual
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — TWO different voice IDs, one per character. Pick by archetype + gender contrast for clarity (e.g. low-male interviewer + bright-female expert). Voice IDs declared up-front in the project, never swapped mid-video.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs Scribe v1 for word-level timing; lower-third with speaker-name color-coded (each speaker = one color). MinimalCaptions style; speaker color is the differentiator.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — ambient professional bed at -22dB, ducked further during speech peaks. Single track for the full duration; the dialog carries energy, music stays out of the way.

constants.structure: 0-3s 2-shot establish + first question → 3-70s alternating turns (Q / A / reaction) → 70-90s closing punchline / takeaway
constants.voiceContrast: two distinct ElevenLabs voice IDs, gender or timbre contrast required
constants.aiDisclosure: small 'AI-generated' overlay top-right for full duration when both speakers are synthesized

variationAxes: with-cutaway-broll (yes — 5-10s mid-dialog visual / no — pure dialog)
VO language (any — English, Russian, Spanish; the dialog rhythm reads regardless)
doNotCopyLiterally: Dialog script, voice IDs, persona archetypes, exact prompts. Vibe / format reference only.
referenceNotes: Reference is OPTIONAL when speakers are archetype-based ('a journalist interviewing a CEO', 'two skincare friends'). Reference is REQUIRED — refuse without it (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) — when either speaker is a real named person, real handle, or specific real entity. No impersonation, ever. AI-improvised likeness of a real person on a real-named brief is always a refuse, even if 'just for a joke'. User can override only with explicit 'generate without reference, I understand the quality will be worse', logged as `stage: "no-ref-consent"`.
